result.plan:
MINIMAL-CHURN SIGNATURE (backward-compatible variadic tail; only the MEMBER Get crosses):

  func Members(store beads.Store, convoyID string, includeClosed bool, memberStores ...beads.Store) ([]beads.Bead, error)
  func TrackItem(store beads.Store, convoyID, itemID string, memberStores ...beads.Store) error

Semantics: DepAdd/DepList/List/DepRemove and the convoy Create stay on `store` (the convoy bead's home store — graph for synthetic, work for user). Only the MEMBER point-read changes:
  - Members: replace `item, err := store.Get(dep.DependsOnID)` (membership.go:102) with `storeref.Resolve(dep.DependsOnID, append([]beads.Store{store}, memberStores...))`. The legacy parent-child List at :69 (ParentID query) stays on `store` (legacy children are always co-resident with the convoy). On ErrNotFound across the whole set -> keep the existing unresolvedTrackedItem placeholder path (membership.go:104-106).
  - TrackItem: replace the existence pre-check `store.Get(itemID)` (membership.go:25) with `storeref.Resolve(itemID, append([]beads.Store{store}, memberStores...))`; DepAdd at :28 stays on `store`.
  - HasTrack, UntrackItem, TrackingConvoysForItem: UNCHANGED signatures. HasTrack/UntrackItem only touch the convoy's home store (no member Get) so they are already cross-class-safe. TrackingConvoysForItem's convoy Get (:160) is the inverse direction (work item -> graph convoy); leave as-is for this phase and handle the two FLAGGED up-walk callers (wisp_autoclose:144, sling_attachment:362) as a bounded follow-up or a second variadic — they are discovery-only with an idempotent degrade, so NOT in the critical drain path.

storeref FITS exactly: PrefixOwner(id,[graph,work]) routes the gcg-/work member by static prefix, Resolve falls back to a full probe and returns ErrNotFound cleanly — a drop-in for the Router's federated member Get. append([store],memberStores...) gives the convoy's own store first then the cross stores; order is fine because a bead lives in exactly one prefix-disjoint store.

SAME-CLASS callers compile UNCHANGED (pass nothing): all of cmd_convoy, cmd_graph, cmd_sling dry-run, sling auto-convoy, every internal/api convoy handler, convoy.go's ConvoyProgress/ConvoyCreate/ConvoyAddItems. SYNTHETIC-convoy callers pass the cross store: drain TrackItem/Members pass the WORK store as the tail (after GE makes drain's primary store the graph store); graphv2 invocation Members/TrackItem pass the WORK store as the tail (convoy on graph, member on work) once the graph store is threaded from PrepareInvocation callers.

ORDERING / DEPENDENCY: this phase is the convoy seam ONLY (the shared primitive). The store-threading into drain (drain primary store = graph; pass work as tail) is Phase GE (dispatch ProcessControl on graph store + drain work-store threading), and the graphv2 input-convoy store threading rides with the graph-create work (Phase GD/GE). Land GC FIRST (additive variadic; zero behavior change at graph=bd and at every same-class call site), THEN GE/GD pass the tails, THEN GF deletes coordrouter. The variadic is a no-op until a caller passes a second store, so GC is independently safe to land and review.

<=5-FILE PHASE SPLIT:
  Phase GC-1 (this seam, <=2 files): internal/convoy/membership.go (add variadic tail + storeref.Resolve on the two member Gets; import internal/storeref) + internal/convoy/membership_test.go (a two-store test: convoy on store A, member on store B, assert Members/TrackItem resolve the member via the tail; assert ErrNotFound across both -> unresolvedTrackedItem). No other file changes — every existing caller still compiles. Verify byte-identical at single-store (no tail).
  Phase GE (separate, <=5 files, AFTER GC): internal/dispatch/drain.go (thread the work store into trackDrainMember/loadOrBuildDrainManifest member-resolution; primary store -> graph) + internal/dispatch/runtime.go (ProcessControl gains the graph/work split via ProcessOptions) + cmd/gc/cmd_convoy_dispatch.go (resolveGraphStore wiring at runControlDispatcherWithStoreAndConfig) + drain_test.go. Pass the WORK store as Members/TrackItem's tail.
  Phase GD (separate, <=5 files): internal/graphv2/invocation.go (thread graph store into CreateSingleItemInputConvoy/ResolveLegacyIssueAlias) + the 5 PrepareInvocation callers (cmd_formula.go, order_dispatch.go, sling_core.go) — split across two <=5-file sub-phases if needed.
  Phase GF: delete coordrouter + relocated-graph conformance test (per G3-OWNER-DECISION).
risks: UntrackItem's DepRemove is SAFE and needs no change: it only DepList+DepRemove on the convoy's OWN store (membership.go:36/58) and never Gets the member, so cross-class never arises. Confirm no future edit adds a member Get there.

SILENT same-class breakage risk is LOW but real: the variadic tail means a synthetic-convoy caller that FORGETS to pass the cross store compiles fine and silently single-stores the member Get -> returns unresolvedTrackedItem -> drain fails-closed with drainUnresolvedMemberError (the exact bug we are fixing, just re-introduced quietly). Mitigation: the relocated-graph conformance test (GF) must drive a real drain over a graph convoy with work members and assert it does NOT fail-closed; green tests at graph=bd are BLIND to this because there graph==work. Adversarial review per the Track-G method.

TrackingConvoysForItem up-walk (wisp_autoclose:144, sling_attachment:362): a closed WORK issue's tracking convoy is a SYNTHETIC graph convoy; after delete the convoy Get (membership.go:160) single-stores to work and returns ErrNotFound->skip, so a live graph input-convoy root may not be discovered for reaping. Stakes are LOW (discovery-only; finalize is idempotent; hasLiveTrackingConvoy returns false->finalize re-runs) but it is a behavior change from today's federation. Decide: bounded follow-up variadic on TrackingConvoysForItem, or accept the degrade. NOT in the drain critical path.

Member Get ordering: storeref.Resolve tries PrefixOwner first then probes all. For a graph convoy with a work member, PrefixOwner(workMember,[graph,work]) returns the work store (work prefix), one Get. For a member with an unknown/empty prefix it probes graph then work — one wasted graph Get. Harmless (prefix-disjoint), but note: pass [graph,work] or [work] consistently; the convoy's own store is prepended so a member that happens to be co-resident is found first.

cmd_convoy.go:262 is cross-RIG (work-to-work), NOT cross-class — childStore is a different rig work store than the convoy's store. TrackItem there does DepAdd on `childStore` (the MEMBER's store), not the convoy's store, which is a PRE-EXISTING latent inconsistency (the tracks edge lands on the child rig store, not the convoy's). Out of scope for the class seam but worth a separate ticket; do not let the variadic refactor mask it.

graphv2 NormalizeInputConvoy/CreateSingleItemInputConvoy receive the WORK store as `store` today (PrepareInvocation gets deps.Store/CLI store); the convoy Create at invocation.go:395 currently routes to graph via the Router/Classify. After delete, Create must target the graph store explicitly — this is a GD store-threading change, not a GC change, but GC's TrackItem tail must be paired with it or the input convoy and its tracks edge land on the wrong store (orphan). Sequence GD's Create-store change together with passing the work tail to TrackItem.

The convoy bead's home store for synthetic convoys MUST be the graph store for DepList/DepAdd/List to find the tracks edges. If GE/GD pass the WORK store as the primary `store` (and graph as the tail) by mistake, DepList(convoy) hits work, finds nothing, and Members returns empty -> drain expands zero units. The convention (G3-OWNER-DECISION / handoff: class store FIRST) must be honored: primary store = graph (the convoy's home), tail = work (the member's home).
orphanPaths: internal/dispatch/drain.go:194 convoycore.Members(store, parentConvoyID, false): after coordrouter delete, with only the work store threaded into processDrain, DepList(parentConvoyID) hits the work store but the synthetic parent convoy lives on the GRAPH store (.gc/beads.sqlite) -> DepList returns no tracks edges OR (if drain is moved to the graph store) the member Get(workMember) single-stores to graph -> ErrNotFound -> unresolvedTrackedItem -> rejectUnresolvedDrainMembers raises drainUnresolvedMemberError (drain.go:282-288) -> the drain FAILS CLOSED (gc.failure_reason=unresolved_member, drain.go:205). Verified by TestProcessDrainFailsClosedWhenInitialMembershipHasUnresolvedMember (drain_test.go:785) and the error text 'unresolved or cross-store member' (drain.go:259). THIS IS THE EXACT FAILURE THE GC SEAM FIX PREVENTS — Members must resolve the work member via storeref over [graph,work].

internal/dispatch/drain.go:889 trackDrainMember -> TrackItem(store, unitConvoyID, member.ID): single-store member Get(member)[graph] fails for a work member -> TrackItem returns 'getting tracked item: ErrNotFound' (membership.go:26), aborting drain-unit tracking. Crosses only after the variadic tail passes the work store.

internal/graphv2/invocation.go:404 CreateSingleItemInputConvoy -> TrackItem(store, created.ID, target.ID): if the synthetic input convoy is created on the graph store (correct post-GD) but TrackItem single-stores the member Get to graph, the work target resolves ErrNotFound and graph.v2 invocation cannot build its input convoy. Crosses only via the variadic work tail.

internal/graphv2/invocation.go:195 ResolveLegacyIssueAlias -> Members(store, inputConvoyID, false): single-store Members over a graph input convoy with a work member returns the member as unresolvedTrackedItem (len==1 but unresolved) -> the alias resolves to a placeholder id or the count check mis-fires. Crosses only via the work tail.
result.summary: The cross-class seam is exactly one shared primitive — internal/convoy/membership.go. A SYNTHETIC convoy (gc.synthetic type=convoy -> ClassGraph, confirmed in coordclass/classify.go:126 isSyntheticConvoy) holds a 'tracks' dep to ClassWork members; Members does DepList(convoy)[graph]+Get(member)[work] and TrackItem does Get(member)[work]+DepAdd(convoy[graph],member). The minimal-churn fix is a backward-compatible variadic tail on Members and TrackItem only — `..., memberStores ...beads.Store)` — that wraps just the two MEMBER point-reads in storeref.Resolve(id, append([]beads.Store{store}, memberStores...)). DepAdd/DepList/List/DepRemove and the convoy Create stay on the convoy's home store; SQLite DepAdd (sqlite_store.go:1080) is a bare edge insert with no member-existence validation, so the cross-store edge is legal. storeref.PrefixOwner/Resolve (storeref.go:31/51) fit as a drop-in for the Router's by-id federation. HasTrack and UntrackItem need NO change (they only touch the convoy's own store; UntrackItem's DepRemove is structurally cross-class-safe). The only SYNTHETIC callers are drain (drain.go:194/872/889) and graph.v2 invocation (invocation.go:195/404); both pass the WORK store as the tail once the graph store is their primary store (Phases GE/GD). Every other caller — all of cmd_convoy, cmd_graph, cmd_sling, sling auto-convoy, and every internal/api convoy handler — is same-class (work convoy + work members) and compiles UNCHANGED passing no tail. The failure being fixed is verified at drain.go:259/282-288 + drain_test.go:785: without the cross-store member resolve, every drain over a synthetic graph convoy fails-closed with gc.failure_reason=unresolved_member. Land GC first as a pure-additive variadic (no-op until a caller passes a second store), then GE/GD pass the tails, then GF deletes coordrouter with the relocated-graph conformance test as the gate. Two FLAGGED up-walk callers (wisp_autoclose.go:144, sling_attachment.go:362) hit a graph convoy via TrackingConvoysForItem's inverse Get with a low-stakes idempotent degrade — handle as a bounded follow-up, not in the drain critical path. Key risk: the variadic makes a forgotten tail compile and silently re-introduce the fail-closed bug, invisible to graph=bd byte-identity tests — the conformance test + adversarial review are mandatory.

callers.detail: The single entry. Receives ONE store and dispatches by gc.kind to each handler, passing that same store down. Today store = controlStoreWithGraphRouting() = policy(Router(work+graph)). All graph-pure handlers (scope-check, retry, ralph, check/retry-eval, fanout, tally, workflow-finalize) operate ONLY on graph-resident beads via this store. In scope: NEITHER work nor graph store is named — it is one opaque beads.Store. The plan: that one store becomes the GRAPH store; add opts.WorkStore for drain. closeOrphanedControl(:145) does store.Get(rootID) — rootID is a gcg- graph root, graph-resident, fine on graph-only.

GRAPH-only. resolveBlockingSubjectID/store.Get(subjectID), resolveScopeBody, liveListForRoot (gcg- root → ListGraphOnly fast path), hasOpenScopeMembers, store.SetMetadata(body), setOutcomeAndClose(body/control), snapshot.propagateScopeMemberMetadata/skipOpenScopeMembers. Every target carries gc.root_bead_id+gc.scope_ref = ClassGraph and is graph-resident. SAFE on graph-only primary.

GRAPH primary + explicit CROSS via opts. setOutcomeAndClose(rootID) and the finalizer itself are graph-resident. The cross-store source-bead chain (preflightSourceBeadChain/closeSourceBeadChain → walkSourceBeadChain) reaches WORK/other scopes ONLY through opts.ResolveStoreRef + opts.SourceWorkflowStores (separate explicit beads.Store values resolved by store-ref) and opts.SourceWorkflowLock — it does NOT use the primary store to reach work. CONFIRMED unaffected by making the primary graph-only: the first hop's currentStore=rootStore (the graph primary, holds the gcg- root), and every subsequent hop swaps to a ResolveStoreRef-resolved store. The fallback sourceWorkflowStoresForLiveRootScan(fallbackStore=primary) only matters when SourceWorkflowStores is nil; under the live config it is always set (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:210).

GRAPH-only. findLatestAttempt, ensureBlockingDependency(DepList/DepAdd), store.Get(bead reload), store.SetMetadataBatch, updateMetadataAndClose, reconcileClosedScopeMemberWithOptions, spawnNextAttempt → molecule.Attach(ctx, store, recipe, control.ID,...). The Attach-created attempt sub-DAG beads inherit gc.root_bead_id (attemptPrefix under stepRef) = ClassGraph. molecule.Attach uses store.Create/Update/DepAdd internally → MUST land on graph. SAFE+REQUIRED on graph-only primary (would ORPHAN on work).

GRAPH-only. resolveLogicalBeadID, store.Get(logical/subject), classifyRetryAttemptWithPostconditions (validateRequiredArtifacts does store.Get(root)+store.Get(source) — both gcg- graph-resident), appendRetryAttempt → store.Create(retryAttemptBead/retryEvalBead) (clone metadata carrying gc.root_bead_id+gc.logical_bead_id = ClassGraph) + ensureDep. opts.RecycleSession is a CALLBACK to cmd/gc (workerKillSession), not a store op. GRAPH-only. processRalphControl runs runRalphCheck (reads subject metadata + executes check script, no work store), then appendRalphRetry clones subject/check/attempt-set beads via store.Create — all carry gc.root_bead_id/gc.scope_ref/gc.logical_bead_id = ClassGraph. REQUIRED on graph-only (3 CREATE sites at ralph.go:432,469,502 would ORPHAN on work).

REQUIRED on graph-only (InstantiateFragment at :174 would ORPHAN on work).

MIXED — the ONLY handler with genuine WORK touches. GRAPH side (graph-resident, on the primary): store.Get(root) gcg-, drain-unit convoy store.Create (synthetic=ClassGraph), molecule.Instantiate(item root, ClassGraph), ensureBlockingDependency, ensureDrainDependencyProjection, persistDrainManifest/updateMetadataAndClose(control bead, gcg-), closeOpenDrainItemRoots/CloseWorkflowSubtree (item roots gcg-). WORK side (user beads, ClassWork, need opts.WorkStore): (1) convoycore.Members(store, parentConvoyID) at :194 — parent input convoy + its members are user WORK beads; (2) loadDrainManifestMembers store.Get(row.MemberID) at :269 — user work members; (3) reserveDrainMember store.Get(member)+store.SetMetadata(member, gc.exclusive_drain_reservation) at :1107/:1121 and releaseDrainReservations store.Get+SetMetadata(member) at :1145/:1155; (4) trackDrainMember → convoycore.TrackItem(store, unitConvoyID, member.ID) at :889 which does store.Get(member)+store.DepAdd(unitConvoyID→member, 'tracks') — the unit convoy is graph, the member is work, so the tracks edge is cross-class; ensureDrainUnitConvoy → convoycore.HasTrack at :872, orderDrainMembersByDependencies store.DepList(member) at :773, drainProjectedBlockerIDs store.DepList(member) at :575.

The cmd/gc caller that builds ProcessOptions and calls dispatch.ProcessControl(store, bead, opts) at :243. It already loads cfg and, per gc.kind, sets opts.ResolveStoreRef/SourceWorkflowLock/SourceWorkflowStores/FormulaSearchPaths/PrepareFragment/PrepareRecipe/RecycleSession. WORK+GRAPH both reachable here: cfg + cityPath are in scope, so resolveGraphStore(workStore,cfg,cityPath,nil) and the work store are both constructible. This is where the graph store is selected as primary and opts.WorkStore is populated for drain.

Builds the dispatcher store. Today returns controlStoreWithGraphRouting(controlBdStoreForRig/City(...)) = policy(Router(work+graph)) when graphRelocated, else the bare bd work store. This is THE chokepoint to change: after coordrouter is deleted it must return the GRAPH store as primary (resolveGraphStore over the bd work backend) while the bd work store is passed separately as opts.WorkStore. file:line in scope of both work (controlBdStoreFor*) and graph (resolveGraphStore).

The serve loop. Discovers ready control beads via workflowServeList(serveQuery,...) then for each calls controlDispatcherServe(cityPath, storePath, beadID,...) = runControlDispatcherInStore. SYNTHETIC(graph) convoy work: it processes graph control beads. The work-vs-graph store selection happens downstream in openControlStoreAtForCity, not here — this loop only carries cityPath+storePath strings. NOTE the discovery query is a SEPARATE store read (workflowServeList over storePath), addressed below.

Control-bead DISCOVERY for the dispatcher serve loop. Uses client.ListReadyBeads() (HTTP /v0/beads/ready) then filters to control-dispatcher-eligible kinds. Under graph_store=sqlite the ready control beads are graph-resident; /v0/beads/ready is being made graph-only/class-aware in G1 (huma_handlers_beads.go). result.plan:
DECISION CONFIRMED VIABLE: make the dispatcher's PRIMARY beads.Store the GRAPH store, and thread a WORK store to DRAIN via a new opts.WorkStore field. This is strictly cleaner than the older graphRoutedStore (Path-B) wrapper because every dispatch handler except drain is graph-pure, so a graph-only primary is correct for ~90% of the surface and the only cross-class consumer (drain) gets an explicit work store — no per-op classification needed.

ANSWERS TO THE THREE CRITICAL QUESTIONS:
(1) YES, the primary store can be the GRAPH store alone. No graph-pure handler reads WORK beads via the federated List/Ready/Get. Every store.Get(rootID/subject/body/logical/voter), every liveListForRoot/listByWorkflowRoot, every DepList/DepAdd, every SetMetadata/Close/CloseAll, every molecule.Attach/Instantiate/InstantiateFragment in scope-check/retry/ralph/check/retry-eval/fanout/tally targets a bead carrying gc.root_bead_id (or a gcg- id) = ClassGraph = graph-resident. workflow-finalize is CONFIRMED unaffected: it reaches work/other scopes ONLY through opts.ResolveStoreRef + opts.SourceWorkflowStores (explicit per-ref stores) + opts.SourceWorkflowLock, never through the primary store; the first walk hop's rootStore is the graph primary (holds the gcg- root). The required-artifact reads in retry-eval (store.Get(root)+store.Get(source)) are also graph-resident (root is gcg-, source is the gcg- root's gc.source_bead_id resolved on the same graph root chain). liveListForRoot's GraphOnlyListFor fast path becomes moot/native: when the primary IS the graph SQLite store, beads.HandlesFor(store).Live.List already hits graph alone — but the GraphOnlyListFor capability probe (runtime.go:441) returns ok=false for a bare *SQLiteStore, so it falls to HandlesFor(store).Live.List which is the graph store's own List = correct. (No List-restore forwarder needed inside dispatch when primary is graph; the List-restore concern in task #3 is for the OTHER List sites that keep a work-primary store.)
(2) Control-bead DISCOVERY does NOT scan one work store. The serve loop discovers ready control beads via /v0/beads/ready (client.ListReadyBeads in dispatch_runtime.go:892), which today returns work∪graph via Router.Ready and post-G1 is graph-only/class-aware. The per-bead processing then opens the scoped control store via openControlStoreAtForCity. All control beads are graph-resident under graph_store=sqlite (they carry gc.root_bead_id → ClassGraph → minted gcg- in the shared .gc/beads.sqlite). So discovery is satisfied by the existing G1 ready-path work; no change needed in GE for discovery.
(3) DRAIN work-touches: add a WorkStore beads.Store field to ProcessOptions (NOT a [graph,work] store-set — the store-set is wrong here because drain's graph ops vastly outnumber its work ops, and the dispatcher would have to classify each call). processDrain selects opts.WorkStore (falling back to the primary when nil, preserving byte-identity at graph=bd where work==graph) and passes it to EXACTLY the four work-touch helpers: convoycore.Members (drain.go:194), loadDrainManifestMembers (member Gets, :269), reserveDrainMember/reserveDrainMembers/releaseDrainReservations (member Get+SetMetadata, :1107/:1121/:1145/:1155), and the member-side of trackDrainMember/ensureDrainUnitTrack/orderDrainMembersByDependencies/drainProjectedBlockerIDs (member Get + member DepList). The cross-class tracks edge (unit convoy[graph] → member[work]) is recorded on the GRAPH unit convoy (DepAdd issueID=unitConvoyID), so convoycore.TrackItem must keep the GRAPH store for the DepAdd but the work store for its precondition store.Get(member) — this needs care: TrackItem does Get(itemID) then DepAdd(convoyID,itemID). Simplest correct split: pass BOTH stores into the drain member helpers and have them Get(member) on work, DepAdd/HasTrack on graph (the convoy is graph). Drain-unit convoy CREATE (synthetic=ClassGraph) and item-root molecule.Instantiate stay on the graph primary.

PHASE SPLIT (the DECISION belongs to GE+GF; ≤5 files/phase):

PHASE GE-1 (dispatch core — 2 files): internal/dispatch/runtime.go + internal/dispatch/drain.go. Add `WorkStore beads.Store` to ProcessOptions with a doc comment ('the ClassWork store for cross-class drain member access; nil ⇒ use the primary store, byte-identical at graph=bd'). Add an opts helper `func (opts ProcessOptions) workStore(primary beads.Store) beads.Store { if opts.WorkStore != nil { return opts.WorkStore }; return primary }`. Rewire processDrain + its helpers to take/derive the work store for the four work-touch sites only (thread workStore param into loadOrBuildDrainManifest/loadDrainManifestMembers/reserveDrainMember(s)/releaseDrainReservations and the member-side reads in ensureDrainUnitConvoy/trackDrainMember/orderDrainMembersByDependencies/drainProjectedBlockerIDs; keep convoy DepAdd/HasTrack and all graph CREATE/Instantiate on the primary). Byte-identical at graph=bd (work==primary). Adversarial review REQUIRED (cross-class drain is the landmine).

PHASE GE-2 (cmd/gc wiring — 1-2 files): cmd/gc/cmd_convoy_dispatch.go. In runControlDispatcherWithStoreAndConfig set opts.WorkStore = the bd work store for drain (and check/fanout if any work reads surface — none found beyond drain). Change openControlStoreAtForCity + controlStoreWithGraphRouting so the returned PRIMARY store is resolveGraphStore(bdWorkStore, cfg, cityPath, nil) when graphRelocated (else the bare bd store), and have the caller retain the bd work store to populate opts.WorkStore. NOTE: GE-2 can land WHILE coordrouter still exists (controlStoreWithGraphRouting can transitionally return the graph primary AND the work store via a small struct/2-return), keeping byte-identity; or land it together with GF. Update the controlStoreWithGraphRouting doc comment (it currently says 'inserts the per-class Router').

PHASE GF (cutover — coordrouter deletion, coordinated with tasks #2/#3/#5): this is the existing G3 in track-g-design / RETIRE-ROUTER-PROGRESS. Delete internal/coordrouter/*, make routedPolicyStore return policy(workBackend) always, drop the *coordrouter.Router assertions in api_state.go (caching-store builder ~:199, closeBeadStoreHandle), delete coordclass.ClassifyGraphPlan, retarget storeref_test.go. The dispatcher path is already class-aware by GE so GF removes the Router with no dispatcher change. Add the relocated-graph CONFORMANCE test (graph=sqlite): (a) a drain item-root Instantiate + retry/ralph/fanout CREATE land on .gc/beads.sqlite (gcg-), zero gcg- on the work store; (b) a drain over a work convoy reserves/tracks/reads members on the WORK store and creates unit convoys + item roots on the graph store; (c) workflow-finalize closes the gcg- root on graph and the cross-store source bead on its ResolveStoreRef store; (d) byte-identical at graph=bd (opts.WorkStore==primary, resolveGraphStore→work).

WHY NOT graphRoutedStore (Path-B): the older track-g-design.md proposes a graphRoutedStore wrapper that classifies every op. That is rejected by the current decision (auto-memory + TRACK-G-HANDOFF §2: 'Class-aware callers, NOT a dispatcher'). Graph-primary + opts.WorkStore is the class-aware-caller shape: the dispatcher KNOWS it operates on graph beads (primary=graph) and explicitly names the one cross-class consumer (drain.WorkStore). No stateful per-op classification, no coordclass.Classify on the hot path.
risks: DRAIN cross-class tracks edge: convoycore.TrackItem(store, unitConvoyID, memberID) does Get(memberID) THEN DepAdd(unitConvoyID, memberID, 'tracks'). The member is WORK, the unit convoy is GRAPH. If both ops use one store, either the member Get fails (graph store, member absent) or the DepAdd lands on the wrong store. Must split: Get(member) on WorkStore, DepAdd/HasTrack on the GRAPH primary (the convoy's home). This requires touching convoycore.TrackItem/HasTrack signatures OR doing the Get-precondition in drain.go before calling a thinner DepAdd. Highest-risk edit in GE-1.

Drain member reservation lands on the member bead (store.SetMetadata(member.ID, gc.exclusive_drain_reservation, ...)). Member is WORK. If this writes to the graph primary it silently no-ops/orphans the reservation (member not in graph store) → exclusive drains lose their mutex → concurrent drains double-process a member. Must use WorkStore. Guard with a test asserting the reservation metadata is on the WORK store.

byte-identity blind spot: at graph=bd, opts.WorkStore==primary==work, so ALL the GE-1 splits are invisible to default-backend tests (TRACK-G-HANDOFF §2 'Misclassification is INVISIBLE to byte-identity tests'). A wrong store choice only manifests at graph=sqlite, which is LIVE on maintainer-city. Adversarial review + the relocated-graph conformance test (GF) are mandatory, not optional.

workflow-finalize fallback: sourceWorkflowStoresForLiveRootScan falls back to {Store: primary} when opts.SourceWorkflowStores is nil. If a code path ever calls ProcessControl on a workflow-finalize bead WITHOUT setting SourceWorkflowStores (e.g. a test or a non-finalize kind that still walks the chain), the live-root scan would run against the graph primary alone and could miss live roots in work/other scopes → premature source-bead close. Under the live config SourceWorkflowStores is always set for workflow-finalize (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:210), but assert this precondition.

GraphOnlyListFor capability probe (runtime.go:441) returns false for a bare *SQLiteStore primary, so liveListForRoot falls to HandlesFor(store).Live.List. That is correct (graph store's own List), but it means the gcg-prefix gate at :442 is bypassed — every root-scoped List runs the plain graph List even for a (hypothetical) mc- work root. Under graph-primary a work-rooted molecule cannot reach this handler anyway (its control beads are graph-resident), so this is benign, but document it so a future reader does not 'fix' it back to a federated probe.

Ordering coupling: GE-2 changes openControlStoreAtForCity to return BOTH a graph primary and a work store. If GE-2 lands before GF (Router still present), controlStoreWithGraphRouting must transitionally produce the graph store as primary WITHOUT the Router while the Router still serves other paths — ensure the transitional shape does not double-open the graph SQLite handle (reuse graphStoreHandleCache via resolveGraphStore, never openClassSQLiteStore/.gc/graph/ — the data-orphan landmine).

Drain item-root molecule.Instantiate (drain.go:940) and the sequential-fallback store.Create inside it MUST stay on the graph primary. If a future refactor accidentally passes opts.WorkStore there, item-root graph beads orphan onto Dolt and the drain never converges. Keep Instantiate strictly on the primary.
orphanPaths: internal/dispatch/control.go:514 molecule.Attach(ctx, store, recipe, control.ID, ...) — retry/ralph next-attempt sub-DAG. If the dispatcher primary were the WORK store, the attempt beads (gc.root_bead_id, ClassGraph) would orphan onto Dolt. Primary MUST be graph. (Safe under the decision: primary=graph.)

internal/dispatch/retry.go:521 + :527 store.Create(retryAttemptBead/retryEvalBead) — retry-eval next-attempt. Clones metadata carrying gc.root_bead_id+gc.logical_bead_id = ClassGraph. Orphans on work-primary. (Safe: primary=graph.)

internal/dispatch/ralph.go:432 + :469 + :502 store.Create(subject/attempt-set/check) — ralph legacy re-spawn. Carry gc.root_bead_id/gc.scope_ref/gc.logical_bead_id = ClassGraph. Orphan on work-primary. (Safe: primary=graph.)

internal/dispatch/fanout.go:174 molecule.InstantiateFragment(ctx, store, fragment, FragmentOptions{RootID: rootID}) — fanout child fragments attach under the gcg- root = ClassGraph. Orphan on work-primary. (Safe: primary=graph.)

internal/dispatch/drain.go:855 store.Create(drain-unit convoy, gc.synthetic=true) = ClassGraph and drain.go:940 molecule.Instantiate(item root) = ClassGraph. These MUST stay on the graph primary even though the drain ALSO touches the work store for members; do NOT route these through opts.WorkStore. (Safe under the decision IF GE-1 keeps CREATE/Instantiate on the primary and only the member reads/reservations on WorkStore.)

NOTE: none of these are NEW orphan paths introduced by the decision — they are the reason the decision (primary=graph) is correct. The orphan risk is the INVERSE: making the primary work would orphan all six. The decision eliminates the orphan by making the graph store primary. The ONLY residual orphan risk is misrouting a drain WORK op (reservation/member) — that orphans a reservation, not a graph bead — covered in risks.
result.summary: The decision is sound and is the cleanest class-aware shape. dispatch.ProcessControl(store, bead, opts) takes ONE opaque beads.Store; every handler except drain operates exclusively on graph-resident beads (gc.root_bead_id → ClassGraph → gcg- in .gc/beads.sqlite), so making the primary the GRAPH store is correct and REQUIRED (a work primary would orphan all six CREATE/Attach/Instantiate sites in retry/ralph/fanout/drain). workflow-finalize is confirmed unaffected because it crosses to work/other scopes only via opts.ResolveStoreRef/SourceWorkflowStores/SourceWorkflowLock, never via the primary. Control-bead discovery already comes from /v0/beads/ready (graph-aware post-G1), not a single-work-store scan, so it is not a blocker. DRAIN is the only cross-class handler: add a WorkStore beads.Store field to ProcessOptions (nil ⇒ primary, byte-identical at graph=bd) and thread it to exactly the member-side touches — convoy.Members (drain.go:194), loadDrainManifestMembers Gets (:269), reserveDrainMember/releaseDrainReservations member Get+SetMetadata (:1107/:1121/:1145/:1155), and the member Get/DepList inside trackDrainMember/ensureDrainUnitConvoy/orderDrainMembersByDependencies/drainProjectedBlockerIDs — while keeping the synthetic drain-unit convoy CREATE, item-root Instantiate, and the cross-class tracks DepAdd on the graph primary. Phase split: GE-1 = dispatch core (runtime.go + drain.go, add WorkStore + rewire drain, ≤2 files, adversarial review required); GE-2 = cmd/gc wiring (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go: openControlStoreAtForCity returns graph primary + work store, set opts.WorkStore); GF = cutover (delete coordrouter — the existing G3 — plus the relocated-graph conformance test). The hardest edit is the drain cross-class tracks edge (member Get on work, DepAdd on graph). Prefer opts.WorkStore over a [graph,work] store-set: the store-set forces per-op classification, which contradicts the 'class-aware callers, not a dispatcher' principle.

Audit checks whether every graph-bead create/mutate goes through a class-aware caller.

result.plan:
Single root cause: post-cutover the generic policy-wrapped store = work-only, and every graph create/mutate that today rides the Router (because openStoreResultAtForCity / controlStoreWithGraphRouting / cr.cityBeadStore() / state.BeadStore wrap in routedPolicyStore) silently lands on the work store. The class-aware seam already exists (resolveGraphStore, cr.graphBeadStore(), state.GraphBeadStore()) and the mutate-by-id read path is already covered (beadStoresForID graph arm). The fix is to route each CREATE site through the graph store before deleting coordrouter. Concrete minimal-churn plan, <=5 files/phase:

Phase GD (molecule callers pick graph store) [<=5 files]: thread the graph store into the create legs. (1) order_dispatch.go: change storeFn/dispatchWisp so prepareOrderWispRecipe + molecule.Instantiate run on resolveGraphStore(workStore,...) while order-tracking stays on workStore; (2) cmd_order.go: use resolveGraphStore for the prepareOrderWispRecipe+Instantiate legs; (3) cmd_formula.go cook: resolveGraphStore for PrepareInvocation+Instantiate; (4) sling: set SlingDeps.Store=graph store for graph.v2 (or add a GraphStore field consumed by InstantiateSlingFormula+PrepareInvocation) at handler_sling.go + cmd_sling.go. This replaces the Router-as-control-store mechanism.

risks: The Router today does CLASS routing; the policy wrapper (bead_policy_store.go) does only TIER selection. Deleting coordrouter WITHOUT first routing every create site through resolveGraphStore is the irreversible orphan event — graph beads written to the Dolt work store are not reversibly relocatable and would corrupt molecule convergence (gcg ids minted by the work store collide with the gcg sequence assumptions).

Several create sites need BOTH stores in scope, not a blanket swap: order dispatch (order-tracking bead on work, wisp on graph), drain (synthetic convoy + item molecule on graph, but tracked work-bead members read from work), sling (source-bead idempotency/existence reads on work, workflow create on graph), convergence (convergence/wisp on graph; any work-bead reads on work). A naive single-store swap to graph would strand work reads.

gcg id namespace: the graph store mints `gcg` prefix; a create that orphans onto the work store gets a `gc`/`ga` id, so downstream gc.root_bead_id/gc.routed_to wiring and bd close-by-id (backendForID) break silently — failures are non-loud (orphaned molecule never converges), exactly the kind the cutover cannot detect after the fact.

POST /v0/beads + bd-shim create is an OPEN create surface: a worker can mint a graph-class bead (type=wisp / gc.root_bead_id / gc.synthetic / type=convergence) through findStore(rig). Unless the handler classifies, this orphans even though every internal caller is fixed. This is the one create path where the bead class is caller-supplied, not statically known.

molecule_autoclose / wisp_autoclose / wisp-GC are MUTATE/DELETE (not create) but on graph-class beads via the work-store accessor; missing them leaves graph wisps un-reaped/un-closed after cutover even if creates are fixed.

ProcessControl threads ONE store to drain/fanout/ralph/attach; the drain work-bead member reads (ListByMetadata over tracked work beads) currently rely on the Router federating work+graph. Moving the store to graph-only without threading the work store into ProcessOptions will break drain member resolution.

result.summary: The codebase achieves graph-bead class-awareness TODAY almost entirely through ONE mechanism: coordrouter.Router embedded in the policy-wrapped store. openStoreResultAtForCity (every CLI store), controlStoreWithGraphRouting (control dispatch), cr.cityBeadStore()/state.BeadStore (controller + API) all wrap the work store in routedPolicyStore(), which inserts the Router when graphRelocated. molecule.Instantiate / InstantiateFragment / Attach / ApplyGraphPlan / store.Create are all store-identity driven — they create on whatever store they are handed — so the Router being that store is what routes gcg-class beads correctly. The policy wrapper itself only selects the storage TIER, never the CLASS. Therefore deleting coordrouter WITHOUT first routing each create/mutate site through resolveGraphStore()/graphBeadStore() will orphan graph beads onto the Dolt work store. Class-aware seams already exist (resolveGraphStore, cr.graphBeadStore(), state.GraphBeadStore()) and the mutate-by-id path is already covered (beadStoresForID graph arm at handler_beads.go:185). The genuine orphan vectors that must be fixed BEFORE cutover are listed in orphanPaths: order dispatch + manual order run, formula cook, API+CLI sling, dispatch ProcessControl/drain/fanout/ralph/attach (Phase GE), convergence (uses cityBeadStore not graphBeadStore), the raw POST /v0/beads + bd-shim create handler (caller-supplied class, must classify), and the molecule_autoclose / wisp_autoclose / wisp-GC graph-class mutate/delete sweeps. POST /v0/convoys creates only plain user convoys (ClassWork) and is NOT an orphan vector. Session reconcilers touch only ClassSessions (already off the Router) and are clear.

CUTOVER PHASE SPLIT (each <=5 files, byte-identical at graph=bd; relocated correctness proven only at the conformance phase):

PHASE G3a — WIRING (<=5 files): (1) cmd/gc/api_state.go routedPolicyStore (:251) -> `return wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies(workBackend, cfg)` always; delete the graphRelocated gate body, coordrouter.New, registerGraphStoreBackend/registerGraphStoreSQLite (now Router-only-dead; their open+cache logic already lives in resolveGraphStore/openGraphSQLiteStore from G0). graphRelocated(cfg) stays (used by controlStoreWithGraphRouting + resolveGraphStore + 3 Ready sites). (2) cmd/gc/cmd_convoy_dispatch.go controlStoreWithGraphRouting (:457) -> when graphRelocated, return policy(resolveGraphStore(...)) as the dispatcher PRIMARY (graph-first) AND thread the bd work store into ProcessControl for the cross-class drain/convoy seam (the only irreducible cross-class site per G3-OWNER-DECISION). This is the load-bearing semantic change: dispatcher runs ON graph; work store is the secondary by-id leg via internal/storeref for drain members. Keep the 3 call sites (:429/:438/:444) but pass both stores. NOTE: this couples to the convoy-membership routing decision (Option A focused graphRoutedStore vs storeref-threaded vs keep-Router-scoped). If the owner picks 'keep Router scoped to convoy seam', G3a/G3b/G3c do NOT proceed — confirm the owner decision in G3-OWNER-DECISION.md is 'delete' before starting.

PHASE G3b — CACHING + CLOSE (<=2 files, cmd/gc/api_state.go only): wrapWithCachingStore: delete the existingRouter assertion (:198) and the existingRouter arm of finish() (:219-222); finish() collapses to `if !policyWrapped {return cs}; return routedPolicyStore(cs, policyStore.cfg, cityPath)`. The relocated graph leg is re-resolved UNCACHED via resolveGraphStore (shared graphStoreHandleCache handle) by the class-aware accessors — never under the work cache. closeBeadStoreHandle (:803): drop the *coordrouter.Router arm (:810-821); confirm unwrapBeadPolicyStore + *beads.CachingStore arms + the noCloseSQLiteStore CloseStore()=no-op cover the relocated graph handle (it is shared/cached, close is a no-op) and the work CachingStore (StopReconciler+Backing close).

PHASE G3c — G2c GRAPH-LIST RESTORE (MUST land WITH or just before delete; <=3 files): the 3 List sites (dispatch/runtime.go:441, session_reconciler.go:2798, pool_session_name.go:198) currently get graph beads ONLY via Router.List federation through the GraphOnlyListFor branch — and that branch is ALREADY DEAD today because beadPolicyStore has a ReadyGraphOnlyHandle forwarder but NO ListGraphOnly forwarder (so GraphOnlyListFor(policy(Router)) returns false; the Router's graph List is reached via the federated List fallback). Post-cutover the fallback is work-only -> graph List visibility LOST. FIX (recommended, mirrors how G1 Ready was already solved): make the 3 sites class-aware by threading the resolved graph store (cr.graphBeadStore() / resolveGraphStore) as a separate param and reading graphStore.Live.List directly when relocated — EXACTLY like liveReadyForControllerDemandQuery(store, graphStore, ...) at build_desired_state.go:1805 already does for Ready (it dropped the GraphOnlyReadyStore interface entirely and calls graphStore.Ready directly). This is preferable to adding a ListGraphOnly forwarder to beadPolicyStore because (a) the wrapped graph store is a raw *beads.SQLiteStore that does NOT implement GraphOnlyListStore/GraphIDPrefix, so a forwarder would have nothing to forward to, and (b) G3-OWNER-DECISION explicitly flags G2c's forwarder as 'goes dead again post-G3 unless G3 makes the wrapped graph store advertise GraphOnlyListStore'. Class-aware-direct avoids reintroducing the interface dependency on a deleted package's shape.

PHASE G3d — DELETE + TEST-RETARGET + CONFORMANCE (<=5 files + the package dir): (1) delete internal/coordrouter/ (router.go, router_federation.go, router_mutation.go, stores.go, bdgraphstore.go, all router_*_test.go, coordtest/conformance.go+conformance_test.go+testenv_import_test.go). (2) Drop coordrouter import + coordclass.ClassWork/ClassGraph uses from cmd/gc/api_state.go (only :201/:220/:348/:362, all removed by G3a/G3b). (3) internal/storeref/storeref_test.go: delete TestResolve_MatchesRouterGet (imports coordrouter); keep TestPrefixOwner + TestResolve_FederationFallback; remove the coordrouter + coordclass imports. (4) Delete coordclass.ClassifyGraphPlan (classify.go:88) + its test (classify_test.go:87) — only the deleted Router called it (router.go:151/167). coordclass package SURVIVES (Classify/Classes/ClassWork used by internal/storemigrate/migrate.go:80/86/91). (5) Retarget cmd/gc test files that import coordrouter: api_state_router_test.go (22 coordrouter refs — rewrite to assert routedPolicyStore returns policy(work) always + the graph store reached via resolveGraphStore), graph_store_resolver_test.go (delete the registerGraphStoreSQLite-vs-opener parity test, keep the resolveGraphStore legacy-location landmine tests), session_router_test.go/cmd_close_test.go/cmd_ready_test.go. (6) ADD relocated-graph conformance (the gate): at graph=sqlite assert (a) graph-plan apply + molecule sequential-fallback land on .gc/beads.sqlite; (b) by-id close of gcg-N lands on the graph store; (c) resolveGraphStore(...).Ready() == graph Ready, excludes work; (d) byte-identical at graph=bd. AFTER the Router is deleted, store=policy(work/Dolt) and GraphApplyFor(work) returns the WORK applier -> every graph-plan apply AND molecule.go's sequential-fallback store.Create(b) (molecule.go:663/890) land on the WORK store = live graph-data ORPHAN. If the owner has not approved DELETE, G3a-G3d must NOT proceed; only G2b (landed) + additive followups are safe. The whole cutover plan is contingent on a 'delete' decision.

Adversarial-review requirement: misclassification (graph op landing on work, or vice-versa) is invisible to byte-identity tests because at graph=bd the two stores are one handle. graph=sqlite is LIVE on maintainer-city (6 rigs), so a misroute ships live. The relocated-graph conformance harness + an adversarial review per landmine phase is non-negotiable (this caught 2 live work-leak blockers in G1).

closeBeadStoreHandle double-close: if the graph SQLite/PG handle were ever returned un-wrapped (not noCloseSQLiteStore) and reached the interface{CloseStore()} arm, the first caller's deferred close would close the shared cached handle for all consumers ('sql: database is closed'). openGraphSQLiteStore already wraps in noCloseSQLiteStore, so the close is a no-op — but the cutover must NOT introduce a path that returns the raw handle.

dispatcher-on-graph semantic flip (controlStoreWithGraphRouting -> graph primary): today the Router made ProcessControl read work primary + graph leg; flipping to graph-primary changes which store ProcessControl's by-id Get/Update/CloseAll target first. The drain/convoy work members must still be reachable (work store secondary via storeref) or control beads referencing work members fail to resolve.
orphanPaths: bead_policy_store.go:48 wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies sourcing the graph applier from beads.GraphApplyFor(store): after Router deletion this resolves to the WORK (Dolt) applier, so molecule.Instantiate/InstantiateFragment graph-plan applies AND the molecule.go:663/890 sequential-fallback store.Create(b) for graph.v2 steps land on the WORK store instead of .gc/beads.sqlite (gcg) = orphaned live graph data. MUST source from resolveGraphStore(workBackend,cfg,cityPath,rec) (G2a) — which is hard-pinned to the LEGACY filepath.Join(cityPath, citylayout.RuntimeRoot) .gc/beads.sqlite, NOT .gc/graph/.

The 3 graph-only List sites (dispatch/runtime.go:441, session_reconciler.go:2798, pool_session_name.go:198): post-cutover GraphOnlyListFor(policy(work)) is false -> they read the WORK store's List and miss every graph-resident (gcg-) member. pool_session_name.go:198 specifically re-orphans the strand-heal fix: an orphan-release write targets the rig Dolt leg whose List never returns the gcg- bead, leaving the orphan unreleasable forever.

Any naive fold of graph into resolveClassStore/openClassSQLiteStore would point graph at .gc/graph/ (the class-store convention) instead of the LEGACY .gc/beads.sqlite — orphaning the live graph_store=sqlite city's data. resolveGraphStore/openGraphSQLiteStore (G0) already guard this with the graph_store_resolver_test.go landmine test; the cutover must keep routing graph through resolveGraphStore, never resolveClassStore.

controlStoreWithGraphRouting cutover pointing the dispatcher at the WORK store (the literal pre-Router bd store) instead of resolveGraphStore's graph store would make ProcessControl read work-only and never see sqlite-resident molecule control beads — graph workflows never dispatch (the original E0 mediation regression). The graph store, at the legacy .gc/beads.sqlite, must be the primary.
result.summary: The entire production coordrouter delete-surface is two files: cmd/gc/api_state.go (routedPolicyStore chokepoint :251, the wrapWithCachingStore Router assertion+finish() :198-222, closeBeadStoreHandle Router arm :810-821) and cmd/gc/cmd_convoy_dispatch.go (controlStoreWithGraphRouting :457, 3 callers). routedPolicyStore collapses to policy(workBackend) always; the graph leg is reached out-of-band via resolveGraphStore (G0, already wired, legacy .gc/beads.sqlite). The hard blockers BEFORE delete: G2a orphan-chokepoint (bead_policy_store.go:48 still sources the graph applier from GraphApplyFor(store) -> would orphan graph applies/sequential-fallback creates onto Dolt) is NOT landed; G2c graph-List restore is NOT landed and is the crux — *beads.SQLiteStore implements IDPrefix() but NOT ListGraphOnly/ReadyGraphOnly/GraphIDPrefix (only the Router does), so the 3 List sites' GraphOnlyListFor branch dies post-cutover. The fix is class-aware-direct (thread a graphStore param and call graphStore.Live.List, exactly as G1's liveReadyForControllerDemandQuery already does for Ready), NOT a beadPolicyStore ListGraphOnly forwarder (nothing to forward to). G2b (by-id [graph,city] arm) is ALREADY LANDED. controlStoreWithGraphRouting must flip to graph-as-dispatcher-primary with the work store threaded for the irreducible convoy-membership/drain cross-class seam (internal/storeref). coordclass SURVIVES (storemigrate uses Classify/Classes/ClassWork); only ClassifyGraphPlan dies with the Router. internal/storeref (PrefixOwner/Resolve) and internal/beads/codectest are deliberately Router-free and survive. The whole cutover is gated on the owner's BLOCKED delete-vs-keep decision in G3-OWNER-DECISION.md.
